ops.text: athome tests/test_research_loop.py races WALL_CANCEL_BUDGET_S=0.6 of real wall clock inside anyio.fail_after(3.0) across ~8 tests (lines ~1182,1255,1292,1592,1609,1624,2172) — under CI/runner load the wall_cancel timer misses its window and infra_events comes back empty, flaking the suite (hit on athome commit 94938ed8, run 29686224216, test_accounting_abort_restart_preserves_prior_sidecar_spend, 3.13 job only; also seen locally as intermittent 1-2 failures under parallel-agent load). Durable fix: inject a fake clock instead of racing wall time, or raise the budget's margin. Repo owner's call — out of scope for the serve-layer change that tripped it.
